rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in component implementation scopes for implementation-level bindings and assignments.
Use assign statements only for existing output features or declared auxiliary variables in implementation scope.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must be placed in valid AADL scopes: type scopes for assumptions/guarantees, implementation scopes for bindings.
Do not reference subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs.
Ensure all visible identifiers in AGREE expressions are declared or available in the current scope.
Avoid encoding AADL properties like timing or execution as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed as typed ports or constants.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};`.
Implementation contracts use assign for binding to output features or auxiliary variables.
Guarantee statements should relate visible inputs to visible outputs in type scope.
Boolean expressions in AGREE should only reference AGREE bool variables or model-provided data ports.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes.
Do not reference AADL property names (e.g., Period, Deadline) as AGREE variables.
Do not encode event ports as numeric or Boolean values unless explicitly defined in the model.
Do not use range comparisons or 0/1 equality on opaque Boolean or enumeration data ports.
